title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to increase and adjust for inflation the above-the-line deduction for teachers.
stage_label: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.
plain_summary: This bill increases a tax deduction that helps teachers and other school workers. Right now, teachers can deduct up to three hundred dollars when they spend their own money on classroom supplies and training. This bill raises that amount to one thousand dollars starting in 2025. After 2025, the deduction amount will go up each year to keep up with rising prices. The deduction applies to teachers, counselors, principals, and aides who work at least 900 hours per school year.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Ways and Means.

sections.title: Section 1  Increase in Deduction for Certain Expenses of Elementary and Secondary School Teachers
sections.text: This section changes the tax law to help teachers. Right now, teachers can subtract two hundred fifty dollars from their income when calculating their taxes for money they spend on classroom supplies. This law increases that amount to one thousand dollars. The law also updates the rules so this amount will go up each year with inflation starting in twenty twenty five. These changes will apply to tax returns filed for the year twenty twenty five and later years. The updated rules use twenty twenty four as the starting year for calculating future inflation increases instead of twenty fourteen.
